title: Berry on Polyester
stainChemistry: Berries (blueberry, raspberry, blackberry, strawberry) carry anthocyanin pigments + tannins + sugar. Anthocyanin is pH-sensitive — alkaline cleaners DARKEN it. On polyester, the standard tannin protocol (cold + Dawn + 3% peroxide + vinegar) works directly. Polyester yellows under heat over time, so hot dryer + residual berry = permanent yellow shadow.
whyThisWorks: Cold water + Dawn lifts fresh anthocyanin + sugar. 3% peroxide oxidizes residual tannin. Vinegar keeps anthocyanin red (light/acid side). Cool wash + air-dry preserves polyester long-term.
defaultAssumption: Polyester garment / blend / kid clothes. Fresh berry = quick win. Hot-dried = permanent.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Flip inside out. Cold water through the BACK. Anthocyanin + sugar flush out fast.
Dawn into spot with soft toothbrush. Cool rinse. Skip OxiClean — alkaline darkens anthocyanin.
Hidden-area test on dyed polyester. 3% peroxide damp cloth 5–10 min. Don't mix with bleach / ammonia / alkaline.
Vinegar rinse. Keeps anthocyanin red (light) instead of drifting blue-purple.
Cool wash. Air-dry. Inspect BEFORE dryer — heat sets residual berry on polyester.

warnings: NEVER chlorine bleach on polyester — yellows fiber
NEVER hot dryer until fully clear
Hidden-area peroxide test on dyed polyester

homeSolutions.agent: Polyester Is the Easy Fiber for Berry
homeSolutions.instruction: Standard tannin protocol works on polyester: cold water + Dawn + 3% peroxide + vinegar + cool wash. Two rules: NO alkaline cleaners (OxiClean / baking soda turn anthocyanin DARKER permanently), NO hot water until fully clear (sets berry + yellows polyester).
Flip inside out. Cold tap water through the BACK 1–2 minutes.
Dawn in cold water, soft toothbrush 5 min. Cool rinse. NO OxiClean.
3% drugstore peroxide damp cloth 5–10 min. Hidden-area test on dyed polyester first.
1 tbsp vinegar + 1 cup cool water, 5 min. Keeps anthocyanin red (light) — critical step.
Cool wash. Air-dry. Inspect BEFORE dryer.

steps: 1. NO alkaline cleaners. NO hot water until fully clear.
4. 3% peroxide damp cloth, 5–10 min, hidden test on dyed.
6. Cool wash + AIR-DRY. Inspect before dryer.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if hot dryer was used on residual berry, alkaline cleaner was applied, or set-in stain weeks old.
warnings: NEVER chlorine bleach on polyester — yellows fiber
NEVER hot dryer until fully clear
NEVER mix peroxide with bleach / ammonia

neverDo: Never hot dryer until fully clear
fiberSensitivities: Polyester yellows under heat over time
Polyester tolerates 3% peroxide on dyed (with hidden test)

materialWarnings: No hot water — sets berry + yellows polyester

escalation.when: Hot dryer used on residual berry, alkaline applied, set-in weeks old.
escalation.whatToTell: "Berry on polyester, [type], age [hours / days], home-treated with cold + Dawn + 3% peroxide + vinegar + cool wash. No hot water / no alkaline. [Residual]."

contextQuestions: How long ago — fresh / overnight / older?
Has it been through a hot wash or dryer?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Blueberry has the most anthocyanin — hardest. Pre-applied OxiClean = anthocyanin darkened permanently. Hot-dried = often permanent.
